data.title: Wall Street ekes out gain, bonds rally after weak GDP data
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages on Thursday ended with marginal gains, amid a bond rally sparked by weak GDP data and more back-and-forth on tariffs. 

The benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) erased an advance of nearly 1%. That climb was driven by Nvidia's (NVDA) blowout earnings report and a ruling by a U.S. court that struck down most of President Donald Trump's April tariffs. A federal appeals court later allowed the tariffs to stay in effect. The S&P seesawed through the session, eventually closing at +0.4%.

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) finished +0.4%, after shedding an intraday gain of 1.5%. The Dow (DJI) ended +0.3%, as a post-earnings slide in Salesforce (CRM) capped gains.

Over in the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields were lower as traders snapped up bonds. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 4 basis points to 4.44%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) also fell 5 basis points to 3.95%.

"Today was another one of those days that shows just how headline-driven the market has become. Even UBS came out, making the case that this will remain a volatile market, as everyone is looking for trade and fiscal headlines, which is a fancy way of saying ‘We have no idea what will happen next,’” Leo Nelissen, part of investing group iREIT+HOYA Capital, told Seeking Alpha.

“In this market, I stick to my strategy, which is building a cash position after having bought the dip in April,” Nelissen added. “If we get more reversals like the one today, I’m putting more money to work."

Before the opening bell, the Bureau of Economic Analysis revised its estimate of U.S. Q1 GDP growth to a decrease of 0.2% from a contraction of 0.3%. 

The GDP print helped bump up Federal Reserve rate cut expectations. 

Earlier, U.S. stock index futures had kicked off the day with a big gain. The advance had come after a federal trade court on Wednesday struck down the “reciprocal” tariffs that Trump imposed on what he called “Liberation Day,” putting an immediate stop to the collection of a sweeping set of levies that the President imposed. But later in the afternoon, a brief order granting a stay on the decision was issued by the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit,  

Meanwhile, AI darling Nvidia (NVDA) rose 3.2% after the firm reported fiscal first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations, despite the impact of added export control curbs to China.

“Nvidia continues to defy expectations, proving it’s still powering the AI wave. Nvidia's blowout earnings reinforce the idea that innovation can still thrive even in a market full of uncertainty,” said Paul Stanley, CIO at Granite Bay Wealth Management.

Salesforce (CRM) closed -3.1% despite a strong Q1 earnings result. RBC Capital downgraded the stock to "sector perform" from "outperform" due to “execution risk” tied to the planned acquisition of Informatica. See more here.

data.title: ScanTech AI receives Nasdaq notices for filing delay, market value non-compliance
data.description:
• ScanTech AI Systems (NASDAQ:STAI) received two Nasdaq notices for non-compliance with listing requirements.
  • The first notice, dated May 22, 2025, relates to the late filing of its Q1 2025 report, the company has 60 days to submit a compliance plan.
  • If accepted, Nasdaq may grant up to 180 days, until Nov. 17, 2025 to regain compliance with filing requirements.
  • The second notice, received May 27, 2025, concerns failure to meet the $50M minimum market value requirement.
  • ScanTech has 180 days, until Nov. 24, 2025 to regain compliance by maintaining a $50M market value for 10 consecutive business days.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Skyworks appoints Robert Schriesheim as interim CFO
data.description:
• Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS) said on Thursday that Mark Dentinger will not be joining Skyworks as Chief Financial Officer due to an unforeseen medical condition. 
  • Robert Schriesheim, a member of Skyworks’ board, has been appointed interim CFO while Skyworks conducts a comprehensive search for a successor.
  • Schriesheim has been a director of Skyworks since 2006 and currently serves as chairman of Truax Partners partnering with CEOs, boards and institutional investors as a private investor and director. 
  • Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS) shares were up 1.01% in extended trading.

data.title: PagerDuty Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 beats by $0.05, revenue of $119.81M beats by $0.83M
data.description:
• PagerDuty press release (NYSE:PD): FQ1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $119.81M (+7.8% Y/Y) beats by $0.83M.
  • Shares +0.03%.
  • Loss from operations was $10.3 million; operating margin was negative 8.6%.
  • Non-GAAP operating income was $24.4 million; non-GAAP operating margin was 20.3%.
  • Net loss per share attributable to PagerDuty, Inc. common stockholders was $0.07.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities was $30.7 million; free cash flow was $29.0 million.
  • Cash, cash equivalents, and investments were $597.1 million as of April 30, 2025.

  • Financial Outlook

    For the second quarter of fiscal 2026, PagerDuty currently expects:

    • Total revenue of $122.5 million - $124.5 million, representing a growth rate of 6% - 7% year over year.
    • Non-GAAP net income per diluted share attributable to PagerDuty, Inc. common stockholders of $0.19 - $0.20 assuming approximately 94 million diluted shares and a non-GAAP tax rate of 22%.

    For the full fiscal year 2026, PagerDuty currently expects:

    • Total revenue of $493.0 million - $499.0 million (compared to the previous guidance of $500.0 million - $507.0 million), representing a growth rate of 5% - 7% year over year.
    • Non-GAAP net income per diluted share attributable to PagerDuty, Inc. common stockholders of $0.95 - $1.00 (up from $0.90 - $0.95) assuming approximately 93 million diluted shares and a non-GAAP tax rate of 22%.
    • FQ2 EPS consensus of $0.23
    • FQ2 revenue consensus of $123.81M
    • FY EPS consensus of $0.93
    • FY revenue consensus of $503.26M

data.title: SA analyst upgrades: NVDA, DJT, VKTX, BRK.A, BEAM
data.description:
Here are the latest stock rating upgrades by Seeking Alpha analysts:

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA). Sell to buy by The Techie. "China remains a significant uncertainty, but robust demand for Blackwell products and global AI infrastructure ambitions support my bullish case. Despite valuation being less attractive than in April, Nvidia's leadership in AI and strong product roadmap make it a buy in my book." 

Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A) (NYSE:BRK.B). Hold to buy by Envision Research.

Trump Media (NASDAQ:DJT). Sell to hold by Mike Fay. "I'm upgrading DJT from 'sell' to 'hold' due to its recent $2.5 billion Bitcoin treasury announcement. Despite my prior bearish stance, DJT's valuation concerns remain, but the Bitcoin move introduces a new angle that could limit the stock's downside."

Viking Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VKTX). Sell to hold by Stephen Ayers. "Viking Therapeutics faces tough competition in the obesity drug market dominated by Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO), with first-mover advantages hard to overcome…Despite some appeal for risk-tolerant investors, the odds are against Viking, so I rate the stock as a Hold, given the challenging landscape."

Beam Therapeutics (NASDAQ:BEAM). Sell to hold by Stephen Ayers.

Criteo (NASDAQ:CRTO). Hold to buy by Sandeep Nital David.

PBF Energy (PRD). Sell to hold by Ronald Ferrie.

data.title: PCE inflation seen ticking up in April, as tariff effects filter into data
data.description:
Friday brings another glimpse into how much of an issue inflation is in the U.S. economy. The PCE price index is expected to tick up 0.1% M/M in April vs. flat in March, according to the consensus of economists. The core PCE price index, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, is also expected to edge up 0.1% in April vs. 0.0% in the prior month.

On a year-over-year basis, headline PCE prices are anticipated to rise 2.1%, slowing from the 2.3% rate in March, while core PCE is expected to hold at 2.6%.

Evercore ISI's Stan Shipley estimates that core PCE deflator increased 0.16% M/M in April. "There are some signs that 'hard' economic data is starting to feel the influence of tariffs and economic uncertainty," he wrote in a note to clients.

"The Y/Y gains for the headline and core PCE deflators likely slipped lower in April. However, those Y/Y gains will start to climb higher when the May/June gains are reported," Shipley added.

The Federal Reserve prefers to use core PCE to gauge the underlying trend in inflation.

Friday's report will also offer more data on consumers' purchasing power. Personal consumption expenditures, or consumer spending, is expected to rise 0.2% M/M, slowing from the 0.7% increase in March. Personal income growth is also expected to slow — to 0.3% M/M from 0.5% in March.

The Cleveland Fed inflation nowcast projects April PCE rose 0.19% M/M and 2.23% Y/Y, with core PCE rising 0.21% M/M and 2.60% Y/Y.

Still, the month's report will only start to show the effects of President Trump's tariffs. The full effect of any tariffs aren't expected until later in the summer, as business inventories that were purchased in February and March will have rolled off by then.

data.title: Baker Hughes, C3 AI to expand joint venture through 2028
data.description:
Baker Hughes (NASDAQ:BKR) +1.6% in Thursday's trading after saying its joint venture agreement with C3 AI (AI) to develop, deliver, and market Enterprise AI solutions to the oil and gas and chemical industries will be renewed and expanded through June 2028.

Since establishing their JV in 2019, Baker Hughes (NASDAQ:BKR) and C3 AI (AI) said they have deployed Enterprise AI solutions to Shell, Eni, QatarEnergy LNG, Petronas, ExxonMobil, LyondellBasell, Flint Hills Resources, and others, delivering efficiency and sustainability gains by optimizing production, reducing unplanned downtime, and enhancing operational visibility.

Under the renewal, the companies will increase market activities, continuing and expanding work across the energy and industrial sectors.

"It's difficult to overestimate the importance of the Baker Hughes partnership to C3 AI and its shareholders," C3 AI (AI) Chairman and CEO Thomas Siebel said, noting the JV has generated more than $500M in revenues from the O&G and chemical markets.

Additionally, Baker Hughes (BKR) said it won an award to supply 16 NovaLT gas turbines to Frontier Infrastructure Holdings to power the company's data center projects in Wyoming and Texas, delivering up to 270 MW of power.
